objective: Test whether a low-node edge/minor ladder profile source creates enough fresh depth-two component-value diversity to increase leakage-clean generated composition support.
non_negotiables: Do not promote a new clean composition shard from a source diagnostic unless selected clean support increases materially.

Do not relax Wave 27 no-reuse leakage gates for positions, decomposition digests, composition digests, decomposition-qualified component identities, component value digests, component value identities, component value pairs, or result value digests.
A learned decomposition-benefit claim remains blocked until a larger leakage-clean split beats deterministic floors and matched no-decomposition controls on the same split.

tasks.prompt: Add report-only diagnostics for an edge/minor ladder component profile source and a combined corner-plus-edge source profile search. Preserve all existing shard commands and prove Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 hashes and replay reports are stable.
outputs: Regression tests pinning profile counts, rejection counts, and selected clean rows
acceptance: The source inventory reports baseline, edge ladder, and combined source counts.
The combined source reports 15 left profiles and 14 right profiles but still only 7 selected clean rows.
The result is documented as a no-go, not a promoted shard.
Pinned Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 hashes and replay reports remain stable.

tasks.prompt: Record Wave 35 as an explicit no-go: the edge/minor ladder source gives a 15/14 profile union but does not increase selected leakage-clean rows beyond 7. Update the matrix, claims, and gates without activating model readiness.
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that the combined source remains at 7 selected rows.
Docs identify component-value reuse before materialization as the active blocker.
Docs state that edge/minor ladder variants should not be repeated as the next scale strategy.
No learned or scale claim is activated from the diagnostic.

tasks.prompt: Constrain the next supply attempt after Wave 35. The next wave must target genuinely new component-value diversity, not simple rank shifts, deeper local-move search, or edge-ladder square moves that reproduce the 15/14 profile and 7 selected-row ceiling.
outputs: Next-wave design constraint in the claim ledger
Verification-gate language preventing repeated edge-ladder no-gos
Owner handoff to topology_engineer and retrograde_engineer
acceptance: The next-wave recommendation rules out edge/minor ladder variants as the scale strategy.
The blocker remains fresh component-value diversity under no-reuse gates.
The claim ledger still blocks learned decomposition-benefit evidence until clean support increases materially.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_35_edge_ladder_profile_supply.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 35 plan is internally consistent.
The combined-source report exposes selected support and rejection reasons.
The committed Wave 35 reports are valid JSON.
